metadata.description: 返回值: `return` returns the result. If there is no `return`, it returns `None`. Returning multiple values essentially means returning a tuple. Python code example for returning multiple values: ```python def sample_function(x, y): return x + y, x - y # Returns a tuple containing the sum and difference of x and y result1, result2 = sample_function(10, 5) print(result1) # Output: 15 print(result2) # Output: 5 ``` In this example, `sample_function` returns a tuple with two elements: the sum and the difference of the inputs `x` and `y`. The returned tuple is then unpacked into `result1` and `result2`, which can be used separately.

data_pointers.title: 返回值

The returned tuple is then unpacked into `result1` and `result2`, which can be used separately.
